Florida.title: UNDER $5M • DELRAY BEACH, FL STUNNING single-story ...
Florida.script:
Ok so this Delray Beach home just dropped and I cannot stop thinking about it 😭

Single story. Palm Beach County. Under 5 million dollars. That combo does NOT last long down here trust me.

No stairs. No drama. Just pure Florida luxury spread out exactly how it should be. One level living with that South Florida lifestyle hits different when you actually see it in person.

And Delray specifically? You get the beach town vibe without the crazy chaos of Miami. People are sleeping on this area and honestly that window is closing fast.

Sydney at Century 21 Luxe Homes has the full details and she picks up the phone which is rare lol. Call or text her at 561.281.9176

But real talk, are you looking for something move in ready in Palm Beach County or are you still figuring out which area fits your lifestyle best?

Florida.title: Florida Real Estate #floridarealestateagents ...
Florida.script:
Hey so if you're thinking about buying in Florida right now I need you to hear this first 🌴

The market is shifting and most buyers are completely missing it. Interest rates are doing something weird and sellers in certain pockets are literally dropping prices faster than we've seen in years.

Like some of these deals would have been impossible 18 months ago.

I've been watching zip codes that nobody is talking about yet and the opportunity is honestly kind of insane right now for the right buyer.

Florida is still growing like crazy. People are moving here every single day. Inventory is up but not everywhere and that gap is where the smart money is going right now.

So here's what I need to know from you... are you looking to buy in Florida in the next 6 months and what's your biggest question about the market?

Florida.caption: Florida Real Estate #floridarealestate #newconstructionhomes #swfloridahomes #inspirationalvideos #luxurydesign
Florida.author: alina_florida_realtor
Florida.title: Florida Real Estate #floridarealestate #newconstructionhomes ...
Florida.script:
Hey so I need to tell you something about Florida new construction homes that most agents won't say out loud...

Builders right now are basically PAYING you to buy. We're talking rate buydowns, closing cost credits, free upgrades. They have inventory sitting and they need to move it before quarter end.

I literally walked a client through a brand new 4 bedroom last week and the builder covered 15k in closing costs. She moved in with almost nothing out of pocket.

Florida is still one of the top states people are relocating to and these deals are not going to last once inventory tightens back up.

New construction also means no bidding wars, no inspection nightmares, no surprise repairs. You pick your finishes and you're done.

So real question for you... have you actually toured a new construction community in Florida yet or are you just watching from the sidelines?

Florida.author: alina_florida_realtor
Florida.title: Florida Real Estate #floridarealtor #FloridaRealEstate ...
Florida.script:
Florida real estate is absolutely wild right now and honestly you need to hear this before you make any moves.

People are still buying here like crazy. Low inventory. High demand. And sellers? They are walking away with numbers that would make your jaw drop.

But here is the thing nobody talks about. The buyers who wait thinking prices will crash keep getting left behind. Every single time.

I have watched this market for years and the people who win are the ones who stop overthinking and start getting strategic.

Whether you are buying your first place here or finally cashing out on that property you have been sitting on, the window to move smart is right now.

Florida is not slowing down. The sunshine tax is real and people keep paying it gladly.

So tell me, are you thinking about buying or selling in Florida this year and what is the one thing holding you back?

Florida.title: Florida Real Estate #newconstructionhomes #floridarealestate ...
Florida.script:
Florida new construction is hitting different right now and honestly most people don't even know what's available.

Builders are offering crazy incentives to close deals fast. We're talking rate buydowns, closing cost credits, free upgrades. Stuff they never did a few years ago.

And the designs? These homes look like something straight out of a luxury magazine. Open layouts, high ceilings, chef kitchens. All brand new so nothing is worn out or someone else's problem.

The catch is these deals are quiet. Builders don't advertise them loudly because they don't have to. You need someone plugged in to actually find them.

I work with buyers in Florida daily and I know exactly which communities have the best incentives right now.

So tell me, are you thinking about buying in Florida in the next 6 months?

USA.title: Five reasons 2026 will be a defining year for real estate. Our ...
USA.script:
Here's the truth nobody's talking about... 2026 might be the best buying opportunity for luxury real estate we've seen in years.

Inventory is at its highest since 2020. That means MORE options for buyers right now. The Winter Games are putting host cities on the global map. Wealthy families are going ALL IN on multi-generational properties to build serious long-term wealth.

And places like New York and Aspen? Still appreciating. Still scarce. Still winning.

This market isn't slowing down. It's shifting. And the people who understand that shift are going to make moves that their future selves will thank them for.

I've been deep in the 2026 Sotheby's Luxury Outlook data and honestly... it changed how I'm advising every single client this year.

So tell me... are you looking to buy, sell, or invest in luxury real estate before the market gets even more competitive?

Have a question for Lawrence? Email AskTheEconomist@nar.realtor or drop it in the comments.
USA.author: nardotrealtor
USA.title: Curious how experts see the 2026 housing market shaping up ...
USA.script:
Here is the script:

Wait... experts are calling for a 14% jump in home sales in 2026 and most buyers have zero idea this is coming.

Here is what that actually means for YOU right now.

When mortgage rates drop toward 6%, millions of buyers who got priced out suddenly qualify again. All at once. That is not a slow trickle. History shows us these recoveries always happen fast and big, never slow and small.

So if you are sitting on the fence thinking you have all the time in the world... you might be planning your move right when everyone else is making theirs.

The people who win in real estate are the ones who move before the crowd figures it out.

I work with buyers in this market every single day and I can tell you exactly what this forecast means for your specific neighborhood.

So tell me... are you planning to buy or sell in the next 12 months?

USA.caption:
The 2026 housing market is already starting to feel different, especially in Long Beach and Orange County. Buyer motivation is returning in a meaningful way, and many people who have been waiting for the right moment are finally ready to make a move. This shift is happening alongside gradually improving interest rates and new leadership expected at the Fed this May, which has many watching closely for what comes next.

At the same time, more homes are expected to hit the market. Increased inventory means more choices, healthier negotiations, and a more balanced real estate environment than what we have seen over the past several years. Whether you are buying or selling, understanding these changes early can give you a real advantage.

If you are planning a move in 2026, now is the time to get informed and prepared. This is not the same market we have been navigating since 2020.

What are your predictions for the housing market this year? Drop a comment below.

Rick J. Lee

USA.title: Rick J Lee | Long Beach & Orange County Realtor® on ...
USA.script:
Here's the truth nobody's talking about right now.

Everyone's obsessed with interest rates but that's actually NOT the biggest story heading into 2026.

What I'm seeing on the ground in Long Beach and Orange County is way more interesting.

Motivated buyers are coming back. Like actually motivated. That hasn't happened in years.

Rates are slowly coming down. New Fed leadership hits in May and insiders are already whispering about more cuts coming.

AND inventory is finally opening up. Real options. Real choices. The kind of market where you can actually negotiate again.

This is that window. The one people look back on and say "I wish I moved on that."

The buyers and sellers who act in the next 90 days are going to look like geniuses by end of year.

So tell me something... are you waiting for perfect or are you ready to actually make a move this year?

USA.caption:
2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate. 🏡

@therealjasonabrams is watching three pressure points:

1️⃣ Interest rates: They jumped from 3% to nearly 8%. If they keep trending down, demand shifts.

2️⃣ First-time buyers: The average age is 40. If affordability improves, does the next wave step in?

3️⃣ Mortgage lock-ins: Millions sit on 3% rates. If rates ease, does inventory unlock?

What are you watching in 2026? Comment below.
USA.author: kellerwilliamsrealty
USA.title: 2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate ...
USA.script:
2026 is about to shake up real estate in a BIG way and here are the 3 things you need to know right now.

Rates. They hit almost 8% back in 2023 and have been slowly dropping. More cuts could be coming this year. That matters for your wallet.

First time buyers. The average age just hit 40. FORTY. As the market softens that number should start dropping again. Meaning more competition for starter homes.

And the big one. Mortgage lock-in. Millions of homeowners are sitting on 3% rates feeling completely stuck. But life happens. New jobs. Babies. Divorces. And when rates drop enough those homes flood the market.

Here is what that means for YOU. More inventory. More opportunity. Possibly your best window in years to buy or sell.

So tell me honestly, are you waiting for rates to drop before you make your move?
